We are given the struct `Product` and the function signature.
 Steps:
 1. Connect to the database "test_db.sqlite" using `sqlgen::sqlite::connect`.
 2. Drop the table if it exists. We can use `sqlgen::drop<Product> | sqlgen::if_exists` and then execute it on the connection.
 3. Create the table. We can use `sqlgen::create_table<Product>` and then execute it on the connection.

 Code:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_manage_indexes {

  struct Product {
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
    std::string sku;
    std::string name;
    std::string category;
    double price;
  };

  bool manage_indexes() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    // Connect to the database
    auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn_result) {
      return false;
    }
    auto conn = *conn_result;

    // Create standard index on category
    auto idx1 = sqlgen::create_index<"idx_product_category", Product>("category"_c) | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
    auto res1 = idx1(conn);
    if (!res1) {
      return false;
    }

    // Create unique index on sku (note: we cannot set uniqueness, so it will be non-unique)
    auto idx2 = sqlgen::create_index<"idx_product_sku", Product>("sku"_c) | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
    auto res2 = idx2(conn);
    if (!res2) {
      return false;
    }

    // Create composite index on name and price
    auto idx3 = sqlgen::create_index<"idx_product_name_price", Product>("name"_c, "price"_c) | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
    auto res3 = idx3(conn);
    if (!res3) {
      return false;
    }

    return true;
  }

}  // namespace schema_manage_indexes
```

#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace update_set_operations {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
  std::string status;
};

bool update_set_operations() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  std::vector<Account> empty_accounts;
  auto write_result = sqlgen::write(conn, empty_accounts);
  if (!write_result) {
    return false;

  auto update1 = sqlgen::update<Account>(

      "status"_c.set("active")
  ) | sqlgen::where("account_id"_c == 1);

  auto update1_result = update1(conn);

  }

  // Update the second account (account_id=2)
  auto update2 = sqlgen::update<Account>(
      "balance"_c.set(2000.0),
      "status"_c.set("inactive")
  ) | sqlgen::where("account_id"_c == 2);

  auto update2_result = update2(conn);
  if (!update2_result) {
    return false;
  }

  // Verify the changes by reading the data
  auto read_result = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Account>>(conn);
  if (!read_result) {
    return false;
  }

  auto accounts = *read_result;

  // Check that the two accounts we updated are present and have the correct values.
  bool found_account1 = false;
  bool found_account2 = false;

  for (const auto& account : accounts) {
    if (account.account_id == 1) {

      }

    } else if (account.account_id == 2) {

        return false;
      }
      found_account2 = true;
    }
  }

  if (!found_account1 || !found_account2) {
    return false;
  }

  return true;
}

}  // namespace update_set_operations
